summary: SonarCloud reports 14 issues including 2 critical (raw SQL concatenation, hardcoded JWT signing key), 5 high (plaintext passwords, XSS sinks, unsafe deserialisation), 5 medium (code smells) and 2 low (duplication). Quality gate is currently FAIL — primarily due to the critical security findings. Two new vulnerabilities were introduced this iteration (a missing CSRF token check in AuthController and a new SQL query in ProductService).

findings.title: User input concatenated into a native SQL query (SQL injection)
findings.root_cause: UserService.findByUsernameUnsafe concatenates the request parameter directly into a native SQL string, allowing arbitrary query fragments.
findings.evidence: String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '" + username + "'";
findings.suggested_fix: Use a parameterised query or a JPA derived method: `userRepository.findByUsername(String)`.

findings.title: Hardcoded JWT signing key
findings.root_cause: application.properties contains a hardcoded JWT signing key. The same key is checked in to source control, so any clone of the repo can forge tokens.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the key to a secret manager or environment variable; load it via @Value("${app.secret.jwt.signing.key:#{null}}") and fail fast on startup if absent.

findings.title: Plaintext password comparison
findings.root_cause: UserService.loginUnsafe compares the supplied password with the stored value using String.equals(). No hashing, no salting, no constant-time compare.
findings.evidence: String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '" + username + "' AND password = '" + password + "'";
findings.suggested_fix: Hash the stored password with BCryptPasswordEncoder and call matches(rawPassword, hashed) at login time.

findings.title: Reflected XSS in /api/comment/greet
findings.root_cause: The `name` query parameter is returned to the client without HTML escaping.
findings.evidence: @GetMapping("/greet") public String greet(@RequestParam String name) { return "Hello " + name; }
findings.suggested_fix: Use the OWASP Java Encoder (Encode.forHtml(name)) or Thymeleaf with auto-escaping enabled.

findings.title: Unsafe Java native deserialisation
findings.root_cause: InsecureDeserializationController accepts a base64 blob and decodes it with `new ObjectInputStream(...).readObject()`. Any class on the classpath is reachable, which is the textbook gadget chain.
findings.evidence: ObjectInputStream ois = new ObjectInputStream(bais); return ois.readObject();
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with JSON (Jackson) and validate the shape; if Java serialisation is required, use a Look-ahead ObjectInputStream with an allowlist filter.

findings.title: CSRF protection disabled globally
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the disable() call. Use CookieCsrfTokenRepository.withHttpOnlyFalse() for SPA frontends.
